Section 1
The core task involves creating a Python function that counts word occurrences while ignoring case differences. This requires normalizing both the input text and the target word to a consistent case before performing the comparison.

Section 2
To implement this, we first convert both the text and the search word to lowercase. We then split the text into individual tokens, ensuring punctuation does not interfere with word matching. Finally, we iterate through the tokens to count exact matches with the normalized search word.

Section 3
def count_word_occurrences(text, word):
    normalized_text = text.lower()
    normalized_word = word.lower()
    import re
    tokens = re.findall(r'\b\w+\b', normalized_text)
    count = tokens.count(normalized_word)
    return count

Section 4
